details.body: finding_summary: The fixture's milestone plan and staged bead graph are disconnected. `scenario_bundle()` defines milestone beads `ms-e2e-scenario.bead-1..3` with titles like "Initialize temp workspace" and "Validate mock adapter flow" [src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:155-202], but `scenario_bead_graph_issues()` and the mocked `br`/`bv` outputs expose `ms-e2e-scenario.root-epic`, `ms-e2e-scenario.task-prepare-workspace`, and `ms-e2e-scenario.task-validate-mocks` with different titles [src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:219-315,342-363]. Plan-aware code only confirms milestone membership when the selected bead matches a proposal by id or title [src/cli/project.rs:1342-1414], so this fixture cannot actually exercise the real milestone-linked path it is supposed to support. affected_files: ["src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs", "src/cli/project.rs"] remediation_hint: Derive the `.beads` graph and mock adapter payloads from the same bead ids/titles used in `scenario_bundle()` so the selected bead resolves back to the milestone plan.

finding_summary: The preloaded `br create` mock returns `Created bead ms-e2e-scenario.task-validate-mocks` [src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:348-352], but that id is already present in the staged bead graph as the existing validation task [src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:275-315]. Creation/reconciliation code resolves the created bead id from `br create` stdout and then inspects that bead [src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:4056-4095], so reusing an existing id makes the fixture unusable for any creation flow and can mask duplicate-id bugs. affected_files: ["src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs", "src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs"] remediation_hint: Return a distinct synthetic id for the mocked create response and queue a matching `show` payload for that new bead, or avoid embedding an existing bead id in the mocked stdout.

finding_summary: `build_e2e_milestone_scenario_fixture()` first materializes a placeholder milestone and then rematerializes the real scenario bundle on top of it. `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:67` creates the seed milestone with `MilestoneFixtureBuilder::new(...).add_bead(...)`, `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:80` writes a different bundle with new bead IDs, and `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:91` returns the journal/task-run state reloaded from disk. Because `MilestoneFixtureBuilder::write_into()` already persists a full plan and journal (`src/test_support/fixtures.rs:585`, `src/test_support/fixtures.rs:615`, `src/test_support/fixtures.rs:698`), the returned fixture carries stale history for the seed `ms-e2e-scenario.bead-*` plan before the advertised scenario plan. That makes the fixture semantically wrong for later e2e coverage that inspects milestone history or lifecycle transitions.
affected_files:
- src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs
- src/test_support/fixtures.rs
remediation_hint: Build the workspace without pre-seeding a milestone, or add a builder path that initializes the workspace and materializes the scenario bundle exactly once so the journal and persisted history only describe the real scenario.

finding_summary: `build_mock_br_adapter()` scripts a single happy-path exchange instead of mirroring the staged bead graph. The returned fixture only has one initial `show` response (`PREPARE_TASK_ID`), then a synthetic follow-up `show`, and the synthetic bead stays `open` even after the mocked `close` because no later `show`/`list` state is updated. Any integration test that inspects `ROOT_EPIC_ID` or `VALIDATE_TASK_ID`, re-reads the created bead, or validates post-close state will either exhaust the queue or observe impossible data. affected_files: ["src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:318", "src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:325", "src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:343"] remediation_hint: Make the fixture's `MockBrAdapter` stateful, or queue enough per-command responses so every staged bead can be shown and post-`create`/`close` reads reflect the created/closed state.

finding_summary: The new smoke test enforces a strict one-second wall-clock budget around real filesystem work. That makes the test sensitive to host load and sandbox performance, so it can fail spuriously on slower CI or Nix builders even when the fixture is functionally correct. affected_files: ["src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:462"] remediation_hint: Keep the fixture fast, but move the runtime budget to a benchmark/ignored perf test or replace the hard deadline with a much looser non-flaky assertion.

details.message: backend invocation cancelled for contract 'final_review:voter' via 'codex'

details.body: finding_summary: The returned `mock_br` fixture is only usable for one hard-coded command sequence. `build_mock_br_adapter()` preloads a single FIFO queue of responses, and `MockBrAdapter::run()` blindly pops the next entry, so any integration test that calls `show` before `list`, repeats a query, or skips one of the smoke-test steps will receive the wrong payload or exhaust the mock. That makes the advertised reusable scenario fixture unreliable outside the one embedded smoke test. References: `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:344-359`, `src/test_support/br.rs:154-184`. remediation_hint: Make the scenario mock dispatch by command/arguments (or maintain command-aware mutable state) so `list`/`show`/`create`/`close`/`sync` work in any reasonable order.

finding_summary: The mocked `br show` payloads do not actually match the bead graph because `br_show_json()` hard-codes `dependents` to an empty array. In this scenario, the root epic should report downstream dependents and the prepare task should report the validate task as a dependent, but any code that consumes `BeadDetail.dependents` will see an empty graph. That can hide regressions in downstream-context and planned-elsewhere logic that rely on reverse edges. References: `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:427-453`, `src/cli/project.rs:377-390`, `src/cli/project.rs:1778-1835`. remediation_hint: Derive `dependents` by scanning the scenario issues for reverse dependencies when constructing each `show` response.

finding_summary: `build_mock_bv_adapter()` seeds only one `--robot-next` response in `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:331-336`. Because `MockBvAdapter` consumes queued responses and then panics when the queue is empty (`src/test_support/bv.rs:163-169`), a second `robot-next` call against the same fixture fails even though the workspace state is unchanged.
affected_files: src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs; src/test_support/bv.rs
remediation_hint: Make the BV fixture repeatable, either by adding dispatch-based matching or by preloading enough identical responses for repeated `robot-next` reads.

finding_summary: The synthetic `br create` path always returns the hard-coded id `ms-e2e-scenario.task-follow-up-validation` (`src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:36,339-374,490-503`). If code under test creates two follow-up beads or retries the create path after a partial failure, the fixture aliases both operations to the same bead instead of allocating a fresh id, which does not match real `br create` behavior.
affected_files: src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs
remediation_hint: Track a create counter and generate unique ids per created bead, or reject duplicate creates explicitly so tests cannot silently pass against aliased state.

[src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:207-266,277-357] The fixture persists a `plan.json` where `ms-e2e-scenario.root-epic` is a normal bead proposal inside a workstream, then hand-builds `.beads/issues.jsonl` by reinterpreting dependencies on that same id as `parent_child`. That is not a topology the real exporter can produce: production materialization creates a separate milestone root plus workstream epics and treats `proposal.depends_on` as blocking edges (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1602-1757`). As written, the plan artifacts and bead graph describe different worlds, so integration tests can pass against an impossible state. Remediation: make the bundle and bead graph derive from the same production topology, or stop reusing the same bead id for both a proposal and the exported root epic.

[src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:109-148,389-400,613-621] The mock mutation path only updates private in-memory state. `create_follow_up`/`close_follow_up` mutate `created_follow_ups`, `list`/`show` read from that vector, but `.beads/issues.jsonl` on disk is never rewritten and `MockBvAdapter` captures a fixed `--robot-next` response at construction time. After a close, the filesystem still shows the original open graph and `bv --robot-next` still recommends `ms-e2e-scenario.task-prepare-workspace` instead of advancing to the newly ready bead. That makes the fixture internally inconsistent for reconciliation/controller tests that combine br mutations with filesystem or BV reads. Remediation: share mutable scenario state across both mocks and rewrite the on-disk bead graph / next-bead selection from that state when mutations occur.

[src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:555-578] `ScenarioBrMock::response_for_call` only accepts one literal create shape (`br create --title=Follow-up validation bead --type=task --priority=2`) and one literal close reason (`--reason=Fixture cleanup`). The real code paths this fixture is supposed to support use different commands: success reconciliation closes with `task {task_id} completed successfully` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:478-480`), and proposed-bead creation goes through `BrMutationAdapter::create_bead`, which can add `--labels` and `--description` (`src/adapters/br_process.rs:1336-1359`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:4582-4590`). Wiring this fixture into the real mutation adapters will exhaust the mock instead of exercising the intended workflow. Remediation: match the production command shapes rather than a single smoke-test literal.

De-duplicate bead-graph serialization between src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs and src/test_support/fixtures.rs. The new file redefines `SerializedScenarioIssue` (e2e_fixtures.rs:873-899), `SerializedScenarioDependency` (e2e_fixtures.rs:901-911), their `From<&BeadGraphIssue>`/`From<&BeadGraphDependency>` impls (e2e_fixtures.rs:913-953), and `serialize_acceptance_criteria` (e2e_fixtures.rs:857-871) verbatim from fixtures.rs (fixtures.rs:66-79 and 211-292). Both paths currently produce identical JSON, but they are on separate evolution paths: `BeadGraphFixtureBuilder::write_into` (fixtures.rs) writes the initial .beads/issues.jsonl using SerializedBeadGraphIssue, while `ScenarioState::persist` (e2e_fixtures.rs:561-578) rewrites it after every create/close/dep-add using SerializedScenarioIssue. If a field is ever added to the canonical serializer in fixtures.rs (a realistic change — acceptance_criteria already has custom formatting logic), the mutation path will silently strip it, breaking tests in hard-to-debug ways. Fix by exposing a shared helper from fixtures.rs — either `pub(crate) fn write_bead_graph_issues(path: &Path, issues: &[BeadGraphIssue]) -> AppResult<()>` or `pub(crate)` visibility on the Serialized* structs — and calling it from ScenarioState::persist. This keeps a single source of truth for the on-disk shape.

finding_summary: The scenario mocks ignore the adapter working directory, so tests built on this fixture will still pass even if production code forgets to point `br`/`bv` at the temp workspace.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:419-425
- src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:575-659
remediation_hint: Thread `MockBrCall.working_dir` / `MockBvCall.working_dir` into the scenario handlers and fail on a missing or mismatched path, or return adapters that are already bound to `fixture.workspace.path()` so cwd wiring is part of what the fixture validates.

`finding_summary`: `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:451-563`, `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:591-610`, and `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:671-677` make `ScenarioState.issues` the authoritative bead graph for `br list/show` and `bv --robot-next`. The mock adapters never reread `.beads/issues.jsonl`, so any code under test that rewrites the export directly will leave the filesystem and mock responses out of sync. That is a real risk in this codebase because reconciliation paths already rewrite `issues.jsonl` directly (for example `src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:1621-1624` and `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:2059-2071`). This breaks the fixture’s stated contract that mocked responses stay consistent with workspace mutations. `affected_files`: [`src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs`]. `remediation_hint`: make `.beads/issues.jsonl` the source of truth for read commands, or refresh `ScenarioState` from disk before answering `list/show/robot-next`.

finding_summary: `build_e2e_milestone_scenario_fixture()` does not actually hand back usable preconfigured adapters. The returned `mock_br`/`mock_bv` are bare mocks (`src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:52-58,103-109`), but every dispatched response hard-fails unless the caller manually rebinds the adapter to `workspace.path()` (`src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:821-833`). The smoke test had to introduce a private `mutation_adapter()` helper and explicit `.with_working_dir(...)` calls to make the fixture usable (`src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:1043-1049,1181-1238`). That is a real trap because the rest of the repo normally consumes these mocks via `mock.as_br_adapter()` / `mock.as_mutation_adapter()` / `mock.as_bv_adapter()` directly (for example `tests/unit/controller_runtime_test.rs:341-345` and `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:16049-16050`). Any integration test that uses the new fixture the same way will fail with `no working directory configured` instead of exercising the scenario. affected_files: `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs`, `src/test_support/br.rs`, `src/test_support/bv.rs`. remediation_hint: expose pre-bound `BrAdapter`/`BrMutationAdapter`/`BvAdapter` helpers from `E2eScenarioFixture`, or make the scenario mocks default their working directory to the temp workspace root so the existing convenience constructors remain usable.

finding_summary: `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:546-565` and `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:951-968` assume every dependency target exists. `next_ready_issue()` treats a missing `blocks` dependency as satisfied via `is_none_or(...)`, so `bv --robot-next` can advance downstream work when a prerequisite row has been dropped from `.beads/issues.jsonl`. On the `br show` path, the same dangling dependency trips `expect("dependency issue must exist")` and panics the test process instead of returning a deterministic mock failure. Because this fixture intentionally reloads on-disk graph rewrites, a slightly inconsistent rewrite can now either unblock the wrong bead or crash the mock adapter.
affected_files:
- src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs
remediation_hint: Require every dependency lookup to succeed before a bead is considered ready, and replace the `expect(...)` in `br_show_json()` with error propagation or a mock exit failure so dangling dependencies are handled deterministically.

finding_summary: `build_e2e_milestone_scenario_fixture()` is documented as a complete fixture for milestone controller / CLI flows, but its `MockBrAdapter` dispatcher never handles `br ready --json`. The controller selection path calls `BrCommand::ready()` before it trusts a `bv --robot-next` recommendation, so using this fixture in those code paths will fail with `mock br runner exhausted` instead of returning the scenario's ready beads.
affected_files:
- src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:50
- src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:715
- src/cli/run.rs:440
remediation_hint: Teach `scenario_br_response()` to answer `ready --json` from the current graph (open, non-epic, unblocked tasks) and add a smoke assertion that the fixture can drive the controller's readiness query path.

finding_summary: The new E2E fixture module is not part of `crate::test_support`, so the requested builder is unreachable and its smoke test never runs.
severity: high
affected_files:
- src/test_support/mod.rs
- src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs
line_refs:
- src/test_support/mod.rs:6-10
- src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:63-113
- src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:1106-1115
remediation_hint: Re-add `pub mod e2e_fixtures;` and confirm `cargo test --lib -- --list` includes `test_support::e2e_fixtures::tests::build_e2e_milestone_scenario_fixture_smoke_test` so the builder and its smoke test are actually compiled.

finding_summary: The shared test-support reverts removed the APIs that `e2e_fixtures.rs` depends on, so restoring the module export would still leave the new fixture uncompilable and unable to keep mock responses synchronized with the on-disk bead graph.
severity: high
affected_files:
- src/test_support/br.rs
- src/test_support/bv.rs
- src/test_support/fixtures.rs
- src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs
line_refs:
- src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:27-30
- src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:99-103
- src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:423-428
- src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:595
- src/test_support/br.rs:113-150
- src/test_support/br.rs:154-177
- src/test_support/bv.rs:122-143
- src/test_support/bv.rs:146-169
- src/test_support/fixtures.rs:379-388
remediation_hint: Restore the dispatch-based mock constructors and default-working-dir support in `MockBrAdapter`/`MockBvAdapter`, plus the shared `write_bead_graph_issues` helper, so the fixture can compile and model `list`/`show`/`create`/`close`/`sync` against live filesystem mutations.

finding_summary: `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:849-852` models an exhausted `bv --robot-next` as a non-zero process failure (`exit_failure(1, "no ready bead")`). Production code treats the real `bv` contract for this case as a successful message-only JSON payload and uses that branch to clear stale next-step hints (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:664-693`). Any integration test built on this fixture will therefore exercise the wrong branch once the graph is exhausted and can miss bugs in the no-recommendation flow.
affected_files:
- `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:849-852`
- `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:664-693`
remediation_hint: When `next_ready_issue()` returns `None`, return the same success payload that `bv --robot-next` emits for "no actionable items available" instead of a non-zero exit.

finding_summary: `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:120-121,389-390` constructs proposal labels as `proposal:{fully_qualified_bead_id}`. That does not match milestone export semantics: explicit qualified bead IDs are normalized by stripping the `milestone_id.` prefix before the proposal label is formed (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:690-710`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1651-1676`). In this fixture, tasks get labels like `proposal:ms-e2e-scenario.task-assemble-workspace`, while production materialization would emit `proposal:task-assemble-workspace`. Tests that rely on label matching or proposal-identity reuse will pass against a graph shape that cannot occur in production.
affected_files:
- `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:120-121`
- `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:389-390`
- `src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:690-710`
- `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1651-1676`
remediation_hint: Derive the proposal label with the same normalization path as `canonical_export_proposal_id` instead of concatenating the stored bead id verbatim.
